root_text.an3.104:1.1: “Lokassāhaṁ, bhikkhave, assādapariyesanaṁ acariṁ. Yo loke assādo tadajjhagamaṁ.
root_text.an3.104:1.2: Yāvatako loke assādo, paññāya me so sudiṭṭho.
root_text.an3.104:1.3: Lokassāhaṁ, bhikkhave, ādīnavapariyesanaṁ acariṁ. Yo loke ādīnavo tadajjhagamaṁ.
root_text.an3.104:1.4: Yāvatako loke ādīnavo, paññāya me so sudiṭṭho.
root_text.an3.104:1.5: Lokassāhaṁ, bhikkhave, nissaraṇapariyesanaṁ acariṁ. Yaṁ loke nissaraṇaṁ tadajjhagamaṁ.
root_text.an3.104:1.6: Yāvatakaṁ loke nissaraṇaṁ, paññāya me taṁ sudiṭṭhaṁ.
root_text.an3.104:1.7: Yāvakīvañcāhaṁ, bhikkhave, lokassa assādañca assādato ādīnavañca ādīnavato nissaraṇañca nissaraṇato yathābhūtaṁ nābbhaññāsiṁ, neva tāvāhaṁ, bhikkhave, sadevake loke samārake sabrahmake sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya ‘anuttaraṁ sammāsambodhiṁ abhisambuddho’ti paccaññāsiṁ.
root_text.an3.104:1.8: Yato ca khvāhaṁ, bhikkhave, lokassa assādañca assādato ādīnavañca ādīnavato nissaraṇañca nissaraṇato yathābhūtaṁ abbhaññāsiṁ, athāhaṁ, bhikkhave, sadevake loke samārake sabrahmake sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya ‘anuttaraṁ sammāsambodhiṁ abhisambuddho’ti paccaññāsiṁ.
root_text.an3.104:1.10: ‘akuppā me vimutti, ayamantimā jāti, natthi dāni punabbhavo’”ti.

translation_text.an3.104:1.1: “Mendicants, I went in search of the world’s gratification, and I found it.
translation_text.an3.104:1.2: I’ve seen clearly with wisdom the full extent of gratification in the world.
translation_text.an3.104:1.3: I went in search of the world’s drawbacks, and I found them.
translation_text.an3.104:1.4: I’ve seen clearly with wisdom the full extent of the drawbacks in the world.
translation_text.an3.104:1.5: I went in search of escape from the world, and I found it.
translation_text.an3.104:1.6: I’ve seen clearly with wisdom the full extent of escape from the world.
translation_text.an3.104:1.7: As long as I didn’t truly understand the world’s gratification, drawback, and escape for what they are, I didn’t announce my supreme perfect awakening in this world with its gods, Māras, and Divinities, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its gods and humans.
translation_text.an3.104:1.8: But when I did truly understand the world’s gratification, drawback, and escape for what they are, I announced my supreme perfect awakening in this world with its gods, Māras, and Divinities, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its gods and humans.
translation_text.an3.104:1.9: Knowledge and vision arose in me:
translation_text.an3.104:1.10: ‘My freedom is unshakable; this is my last rebirth; now there’ll be no more future lives.’”
